title: Email addresses and URLs
text: alex.smith@work.com https://example.com/page?id=42 user_name#1234 visit www.site.org and ftp://server

tips.text: An address has to be exact. One wrong character and the message never arrives - user@domain.com, set down whole.
A URL is the same discipline: https://site.com/path?param=value, every mark in its place.
A handle with a tag - user#1234 - reads as one unit. Type it as one, without slowing to check.

character_tips.anna: Addresses and links are the plumbing of a working day. Get them right and nothing leaks.
character_tips.maxim: Type a URL without looking down - the moment your eyes drop, you lose your place in it.
character_tips.knopych: The @ and the .com, laid down clean. That's the whole trick.
character_tips.klavochka: An address on an envelope had to be exact too. This is the same care, newer paper.
